title: GPT-5.4, The Lobster, and The End of the World
tweets:
🦞 EP.003: "Will the Lobster Take Your Job?"

GPT-5.4 drops. An AI project called 'Lobster' (OpenClaw) gets 240K GitHub stars in 2 weeks. A 7,000-word essay crashes the stock market by 800 Dow points.

My daughter: "Dad... I graduate in exactly that year."

Me: speechless. 🧵

🤖 GPT-5.4's numbers made me stop:

• 1M token context (= reading 10 novels simultaneously)
• Direct computer control: mouse, browser, forms
• Beats real humans in 44 professions — 83% of the time

The other 17%? That's the most important number.
That's where humans live.

📉 "The 2028 Global Intelligence Crisis" essay:

→ AI profits skip workers → Ghost GDP
→ Unemployment spiral begins
→ Prediction: S&P drops 38%, unemployment hits 10.2% by June 2028

It triggered an 800-point Dow drop.

But here's the twist: the essay ITSELF is proof humans still matter. 🌀

Two people. Seven thousand words. "We couldn't NOT write it."

AI can write the words. It can't generate that compulsion.

🔥 My 3 Contrarian Bets on the AI Age:

1️⃣ The biggest opportunity: building the PIPELINE between AI and the physical world. AI is stuck in data. It doesn't know your grandmother's chair, your 4pm sunlight, the smell of your home.

2️⃣ The most valuable human trait: curiosity. Not skills. Not efficiency. The irrational question only YOU would ask.

3️⃣ LINGJI: Human irrationality = the universe's only true random = evolution's engine.

AI replaces 83%. The 17% who generate new variables? Irreplaceable.
